analysis.expectedGain: Expected weight gain so far: ~{{kg}} kg
analysis.bpHigh: Blood pressure is high - consult your doctor
analysis.bpLow: Blood pressure is low - increase fluids and rest
analysis.mentalSupport: Mental support is important - talk to someone you trust
analysis.enterData: Enter your data for smart analysis

nutrition.title: Smart Nutrition Plan
nutrition.planForWeek: Customized plan for week {{week}} of pregnancy

meals.breakfastSuggestion: Boiled eggs + whole wheat bread + fresh orange juice

footer.disclaimerText: This app is for educational and informational purposes only and does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for any health concerns.

dueDatePage.title: Due Date Calculator
dueDatePage.subtitle: Estimate when your baby will arrive
dueDatePage.lmpLabel: First Day of Last Menstrual Period
dueDatePage.info: Only about 5% of babies are born on their exact due date. Most are born within 2 weeks before or after. Your healthcare provider may adjust your due date based on ultrasound measurements.

kickCounterPage.title: Kick Counter
kickCounterPage.subtitle: Count 10 kicks to monitor fetal activity
kickCounterPage.goal: Goal: 10 kicks in 2 hours or less
kickCounterPage.info: Starting at 28 weeks, count fetal movements daily. Contact your healthcare provider if you notice decreased movement or don't reach 10 kicks in 2 hours.

contractionPage.title: Contraction Timer
contractionPage.subtitle: Track duration and frequency of contractions
contractionPage.callProviderDesc: Contractions are 5 minutes apart and lasting 45+ seconds. Consider heading to the hospital.
contractionPage.rule511Desc: Call your provider when contractions are 5 minutes apart, lasting 1 minute each, for at least 1 hour.

preeclampsiaPage.title: Preeclampsia Risk Assessment
preeclampsiaPage.subtitle: Check your preeclampsia risk factors
preeclampsiaPage.disclaimer: This is a preliminary assessment only. Always consult your healthcare provider.

fitnessPage.title: AI Fitness Coach
fitnessPage.disclaimer: Consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program.

forbiddenFoodsPage.title: Foods to Avoid
forbiddenFoodsPage.disclaimer: This is a general guide. Always consult your healthcare provider about dietary restrictions specific to your pregnancy.

nutrition.addFoodFirst: Add at least one food item
nutrition.logMealFirst: Log at least one meal first
nutrition.weekInfo: Week {{week}} - Log your meals and get AI tips
nutrition.tip1: Eat 5-6 small meals instead of 3 large ones
nutrition.tip3: Avoid raw foods and unpasteurized cheeses
nutrition.tip4: Focus on protein, iron, and calcium

settings.title: Settings
settings.privacyPoint1: All your health data is stored locally on your device only
settings.privacyPoint2: We don't collect or share your personal information
settings.privacyPoint3: You can export your data anytime
settings.medicalNote: This app is for educational purposes only and does not replace medical advice

encryption.title: Data Encryption
encryption.description: Protect your health data with AES-256 encryption
encryption.enabledAlert: Your health data is protected with AES-256-GCM encryption. The encryption key is stored only on your device.
encryption.disabledAlert: Encryption is disabled. Your data is stored in readable format on the device.
encryption.securityNoteText: The encryption key is stored only on your device. If you clear app data, you will lose access to encrypted data. Make regular backups.
encryption.enabledToastDescNew: New data will be encrypted automatically

backup.title: Data Backup
backup.description: Save your data as a JSON file to restore later
backup.importantText: Save the backup file in a safe place like Google Drive, iCloud, or email it to yourself.
backup.importDescription: Choose a backup file (.json) to restore your data
backup.importWarningText: Current data will be replaced with imported data
backup.exportErrorDesc: An error occurred while exporting data

clearData.title: Delete All Data
clearData.description: Remove all data saved on this device
clearData.warningText: This action cannot be undone! All pregnancy records, appointments, photos, and settings will be deleted.
clearData.confirmWarning: This action is final and cannot be undone!
clearData.deleteErrorDesc: An error occurred while deleting data
clearData.tipText: Export a backup before deleting to save your data

babyGrowth.noData: Add at least one measurement to view the chart
babyGrowth.title: Weight Growth Curve - WHO Standards

dueDate.calculateDesc: Estimate when your baby will arrive
dueDate.lmpLabel: First Day of Last Menstrual Period
dueDate.savedDesc: Your due date has been saved.
dueDate.reminderSetDesc: We'll remind you about your due date: {{date}}
dueDate.copiedDesc: Due date info copied to clipboard.
dueDate.info: Only about 5% of babies are born on their exact due date. Most are born within 2 weeks before or after. Your healthcare provider may adjust your due date based on ultrasound measurements.

kickCounter.title: Smart Kick Counter
kickCounter.subtitle: Week {{week}} - AI-Powered Movement Analysis
kickCounter.basedOnSessions: Based on {{count}} sessions • Updated after each session
kickCounter.tapCircle: Tap the circle for each movement
kickCounter.notesPlaceholder: Notes (baby's position, time of day, after eating, etc.)...
kickCounter.sessionStartedDesc: Tap the screen each time you feel your baby move

symptomAnalyzer.title: AI Symptom Analyzer
symptomAnalyzer.subtitle: Educational insights for pregnancy symptoms
symptomAnalyzer.disclaimer: This tool provides educational information only. Always consult your healthcare provider for medical advice.
symptomAnalyzer.discussWithProvider: Discuss with your healthcare provider for personalized advice
symptomAnalyzer.remember: Remember: Every pregnancy is unique. These insights are for educational purposes only and should not replace professional medical advice.

vitaminTracker.title: Vitamin Tracker
vitaminTracker.subtitle: Track your daily prenatal vitamins
vitaminTracker.tipContent: Take iron with vitamin C (orange juice) for better absorption, and avoid taking it with calcium or tea.

fitnessCoach.title: AI Fitness Coach
fitnessCoach.subtitle: Safe exercises customized for your pregnancy stage
fitnessCoach.disclaimer: Consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program.
fitnessCoach.fitnessVideosSubtitle: Safe workout guides for every trimester
fitnessCoach.safetyText: Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or shortness of breath. Keep water nearby.

exerciseDescs.prenatalSquatsDesc: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Lower gently as if sitting in a chair.
exerciseDescs.birdDogDesc: On hands and knees, extend opposite arm and leg. Hold for balance.
exerciseDescs.pelvicTiltsDesc: Gently rock your pelvis forward and back to relieve lower back pressure.
exerciseDescs.wallPushupsDesc: Stand arm-length from wall. Lean in and push back out.
exerciseDescs.butterflyStretchDesc: Sit with feet together, knees out. Gently lean forward.
exerciseDescs.seatedMarchingDesc: Sit on a stable chair or ball. Lift knees alternately.

birthPlan.subtitle: Create your personalized birth plan
birthPlan.storageFullDesc: Maximum 9 plans. Delete old plans to save new ones.

hospitalBag.subtitle: Smart packing list for delivery day

partnerGuide.subtitle: Support tips for partners and spouses
partnerGuide.whatHelpWith: What do you need help with?
partnerGuide.action1: Ask "How are you feeling today?" and truly listen
partnerGuide.action2: Offer a foot or back massage
partnerGuide.action3: Take over a household task without being asked
partnerGuide.action4: Read to the baby bump together
partnerGuide.partnerVideosSubtitle: Learn how to be the best support partner

laborTracker.title: AI Labor Progress Tracker
laborTracker.subtitle: Track contractions and monitor labor progress
laborTracker.timeToGo: Time to go to the hospital
laborTracker.rule511Desc: Go to the hospital when contractions are 5 minutes apart, last 1 minute each, and continue for 1 hour.
laborTracker.hospitalAlert: Time to Go to the Hospital!
laborTracker.hospitalAlertDesc: Based on the 5-1-1 rule, your contractions are now 5 minutes apart and lasting 1 minute or more. This indicates active labor.
laborTracker.disclaimer: This tool is for informational purposes only. Always follow your healthcare provider's guidance about when to go to the hospital.

birthStory.title: AI Birth Story Generator
birthStory.subtitle: Create a beautiful keepsake story of your baby's arrival
birthStory.firstFeeling: First feeling when you saw your baby
birthStory.specialMomentsPlaceholder: Any special details, who was there, funny moments, etc...
birthStory.storyReady: Your beautiful birth story is ready.
birthStory.storySaved: Your birth story has been saved.
birthStory.keepsakeDesc: This story can be saved, printed, and included in a baby book or shared with family and friends.

stressRelief.title: AI Stress Relief Coach
stressRelief.subtitle: Guided relaxation techniques for pregnancy
stressRelief.tip2: Step outside for fresh air if possible
stressRelief.tip3: Drink a glass of water slowly
stressRelief.tip4: Call someone who makes you smile
stressRelief.tip5: Put your hand on your belly and connect with baby
stressRelief.disclaimer: These exercises are for general relaxation. If you experience severe anxiety or depression, please consult your healthcare provider.

affirmations.1: I am capable of handling whatever comes my way
affirmations.2: My body knows how to grow this baby perfectly
affirmations.3: I trust my body and my instincts
affirmations.4: I am surrounded by love and support
affirmations.5: Each breath brings calm and peace
affirmations.6: I am strong, I am brave, I am enough
affirmations.7: My baby feels my love and calm energy
affirmations.8: I release worry and embrace peace

boxBreathing.name: Box Breathing
boxBreathing.desc: A calming technique used by Navy SEALs
steps: Breathe in slowly for 4 seconds
Hold your breath for 4 seconds
Exhale slowly for 4 seconds
Hold for 4 seconds
Repeat the cycle

safePlace.name: Safe Place Visualization
steps: Close your eyes and take deep breaths
Imagine a place where you feel completely safe
Notice the colors, sounds, and smells
Feel the peace and comfort surrounding you
Stay here as long as you need

grounding54321.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
grounding54321.desc: A sensory technique to reduce anxiety
steps: Notice 5 things you can SEE
Notice 4 things you can TOUCH
Notice 3 things you can HEAR
Notice 2 things you can SMELL
Notice 1 thing you can TASTE

progressiveRelaxation.name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
progressiveRelaxation.desc: Release tension from head to toe
steps: Start with your feet - tense for 5 seconds, release
Move to your calves - tense and release
Continue to thighs, abdomen, hands, arms
Tense and release shoulders, neck, face
Feel the wave of relaxation through your body

mobilityCoach.title: AI Mobility Coach
mobilityCoach.subtitle: Walking tips & leg cramp prevention for pregnancy
mobilityCoach.startWalkDesc: Start a gentle walk for better circulation
mobilityCoach.tip1: Walk for 20-30 minutes daily at a comfortable pace
mobilityCoach.tip2: Stay hydrated before, during, and after walks
mobilityCoach.tip4: Do calf stretches before bed to prevent night cramps
mobilityCoach.tip5: Keep magnesium-rich foods in your diet

calfRaises.name: Calf Raises
calfRaises.desc: Stand and raise heels off ground, lower slowly

ankleCircles.name: Ankle Circles
ankleCircles.desc: Rotate each ankle in circles, both directions

toePoints.name: Toe Points
toePoints.desc: Point and flex toes to improve circulation

postureCoach.title: AI Posture Coach
postureCoach.subtitle: Improve pregnancy posture to reduce back pain and discomfort
postureCoach.warning: Stop any exercise if you feel pain, dizziness, or discomfort. Consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise routine.

massageGuide.title: Pregnancy Massage Guide
massageGuide.subtitle: Safe massage techniques for pregnancy comfort
massageGuide.safety3: Stop if you feel any discomfort

mentalHealth.title: Mental Health Coach
mentalHealth.subtitle: EPDS screening and mental health support
mentalHealth.crisis: If you're in crisis, call your healthcare provider or emergency services immediately.

weeklyInsights.title: Weekly Insights
weeklyInsights.subtitle: Smart weekly updates on your baby's growth

gestationalDiabetes.subtitle: Know your gestational diabetes risk factors

cycleTracker.title: Cycle & Ovulation Tracker
cycleTracker.subtitle: Track your cycle and find your fertile days

bmi.title: Pregnancy BMI Calculator
bmi.subtitle: Calculate your pre-pregnancy BMI and weight gain recommendations
bmi.disclaimer: These recommendations are based on Institute of Medicine guidelines. Your healthcare provider may give personalized advice based on your specific health situation and pregnancy.

gdm.title: Gestational Diabetes Risk
gdm.subtitle: Assess your risk factors for gestational diabetes
gdm.disclaimer: This is an educational screening tool only. It does not diagnose gestational diabetes. Always consult your healthcare provider for proper testing and diagnosis.
gdm.infoNote: Gestational diabetes screening typically occurs between weeks 24-28. If you have multiple risk factors, your doctor may recommend earlier screening. This tool is for educational purposes only and does not replace medical advice.

high.title: Higher Risk
high.description: Your risk factors suggest elevated likelihood. Early screening may be recommended.

moderate.title: Moderate Risk
moderate.description: You have some risk factors. Standard screening at 24-28 weeks is recommended.

low.title: Lower Risk
low.description: You have few risk factors, but routine screening at 24-28 weeks is still recommended.

preventionTips.tip1: Maintain a healthy weight before and during pregnancy
preventionTips.tip2: Eat a balanced diet rich in fiber and whole grains
preventionTips.tip3: Stay physically active with doctor-approved exercises
preventionTips.tip4: Attend all prenatal appointments for regular monitoring
preventionTips.tip5: Limit sugary foods and refined carbohydrates

walkingCoach.title: Smart Walking Coach
walkingCoach.subtitle: Personalized walking program for a healthy pregnancy

walkingTips.tip4: Walk during cooler parts of the day

walkingCoach.warningMessage: Stop walking if you experience dizziness, shortness of breath, chest pain, contractions, or any unusual symptoms. Always consult your healthcare provider.

trimesterNotes.1: Start slowly, listen to your body

nutritionOptimizer.title: Smart Nutrition Optimizer
nutritionOptimizer.subtitle: Personalized meal planning for your pregnancy week
nutritionOptimizer.infoNote: These are general recommendations. Always consult with your healthcare provider for personalized nutrition advice during pregnancy.

snackPlanner.title: Smart Snack Planner
snackPlanner.subtitle: Healthy pregnancy snack ideas
snackPlanner.letAIPick: Let AI pick a perfect snack for you

snackingTips.tip1: Eat every 2-3 hours to maintain energy
snackingTips.tip2: Keep snacks by your bedside for morning sickness
snackingTips.tip4: Listen to your cravings but balance with nutrition

workoutPlanner.title: Personalized Workout Planner
workoutPlanner.subtitle: Safe pregnancy exercises for each trimester
workoutPlanner.safetyNotice: Always get clearance from your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program during pregnancy.
workoutPlanner.greatJob: Great job taking care of yourself and your baby!

mentalHealthCoach.title: Postpartum Mental Health Coach
mentalHealthCoach.subtitle: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale screening
mentalHealthCoach.emergencyMessage: Based on your responses, it's important to talk to someone who can help. Postpartum depression is common and treatable.
mentalHealthCoach.question: Question
mentalHealthCoach.screeningDisclaimer: ⚠️ This is a screening tool based on the Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS). It does not provide a diagnosis. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper evaluation.

low.title: Low Risk
low.message: Your responses suggest you're coping well. Continue practicing self-care.

moderate.title: Moderate
moderate.message: Consider speaking with your healthcare provider about how you're feeling.

high.title: Seek Support
high.message: Please reach out to your healthcare provider or a mental health professional.

welcome.title: Welcome to Pregnancy Toolkits
welcome.subtitle: Your complete pregnancy companion
welcome.disclaimerText: This app provides general information only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice. Always consult your healthcare provider for any health concerns.
welcome.agreement: By continuing, you agree to our Terms of Service

preeclampsiaRisk.title: Preeclampsia Risk Calculator
preeclampsiaRisk.subtitle: Evaluate your risk factors for preeclampsia
preeclampsiaRisk.riskFactorsDesc: Check all factors that apply to evaluate your risk level
preeclampsiaRisk.warningNote: If you experience any of these symptoms, contact your healthcare provider immediately.
preeclampsiaRisk.disclaimer: This tool identifies risk factors but cannot predict or diagnose preeclampsia. Regular prenatal care with blood pressure monitoring is essential for all pregnancies.

high.title: Higher Risk
high.description: You have one or more significant risk factors for preeclampsia. Your provider may recommend low-dose aspirin starting at 12 weeks.
high.recommendation: Discuss aspirin prophylaxis with your healthcare provider

moderate.title: Moderate Risk
moderate.description: You have some risk factors. Enhanced monitoring and lifestyle modifications may be recommended.
moderate.recommendation: Consider discussing risk with your healthcare provider

low.title: Lower Risk
low.description: You have few identified risk factors. Continue with standard prenatal care.

warningSigns.headaches: Severe headaches that don't go away
warningSigns.weightGain: Sudden weight gain (more than 5 lbs in a week)

t1.detail: Aim for 8-10 glasses of water daily to support amniotic fluid and reduce swelling.

t2.detail: Leafy greens like spinach and kale are packed with folate essential for neural development.

t3.detail: Walking improves circulation, reduces swelling, and helps with labor preparation.

t4.detail: Write down 3 things you're grateful for today. This reduces stress and improves mood.

t5.detail: Your baby can hear you! Reading or singing strengthens your bond.

t6.detail: This position improves blood flow to your baby and reduces pressure on organs.

t7.detail: Salmon, walnuts, and chia seeds support baby's brain development.

t8.detail: Pelvic floor exercises prepare you for labor and prevent incontinence.

t9.detail: Join a prenatal class or online community for support and shared experiences.

t10.detail: After week 28, track baby movements. You should feel 10 kicks in 2 hours.

t11.detail: Reduce swelling by putting your feet up for 15 minutes several times a day.

t12.detail: A protein-rich breakfast stabilizes blood sugar and reduces nausea.

t13.detail: Gentle stretching relieves back pain and prepares your body for labor.

t14.detail: Step away from screens for 30 minutes to reduce stress and eye strain.

t15.detail: Play calming music for baby - they'll recognize it after birth!

nauseaRelief.title: AI Nausea Relief Coach
nauseaRelief.subtitle: Personalized relief strategies for morning sickness
nauseaRelief.vomitingCheckbox: I'm experiencing vomiting, not just nausea
nauseaRelief.severeWarningDesc: If you can't keep any food/water down for 24 hours, see blood in vomit, or feel dizzy/faint, contact your healthcare provider immediately.
nauseaRelief.encouragingNote: Remember: Morning sickness usually peaks around weeks 8-10 and improves significantly by week 12-14. You've got this!
nauseaRelief.videosSubtitle: Techniques and tips to ease morning sickness

legCrampPreventer.title: AI Leg Cramp Preventer
legCrampPreventer.subtitle: Prevent and manage pregnancy leg cramps with smart tips
legCrampPreventer.warning: Frequent or severe leg cramps, especially with swelling, redness, or warmth, should be reported to your healthcare provider immediately.

step2.title: Massage the muscle
step2.desc: Rub gently in the direction of the cramp

step3.title: Apply heat
step3.desc: Use a warm towel or heating pad

step4.title: Walk around
step4.desc: Gentle movement helps release the cramp

hydrated.title: Stay Hydrated
hydrated.desc: Drink at least 8-10 glasses of water daily

stretch.title: Stretch Before Bed
stretch.desc: Gentle calf stretches can prevent nighttime cramps

magnesium.title: Magnesium-Rich Foods
magnesium.desc: Eat nuts, seeds, bananas, and leafy greens

standing.title: Avoid Standing Too Long
standing.desc: Take breaks and elevate your legs when possible

sleep.title: Sleep Position
sleep.desc: Sleep on your left side with pillow between knees

toes.title: Avoid Pointing Toes
toes.desc: Flex feet instead of pointing when stretching

weeklySummary.title: Weekly Insights
weeklySummary.subtitle: Smart weekly tracking for your pregnancy
weeklySummary.trackWeekTip: Track your week to get personalized information about your baby's growth and your body changes

pregnancyAssistant.title: Smart Pregnancy Guide
pregnancyAssistant.subtitle: Your 24/7 pregnancy companion
pregnancyAssistant.askAnything: Ask anything about pregnancy, nutrition, or baby care

lactationPrep.title: AI Lactation Preparation
lactationPrep.subtitle: Your breastfeeding preparation guide
lactationPrep.firstTimeBreastfeeding: This is my first time breastfeeding
lactationPrep.supportNote: If you're struggling with breastfeeding, reach out to a lactation consultant (IBCLC). Many hospitals offer free support. Fed is best - whatever works for you and baby!

backPainRelief.title: AI Back Pain Relief
backPainRelief.subtitle: Pregnancy-safe exercises for back pain relief
backPainRelief.safetyWarning: Stop immediately if you experience sharp pain, dizziness, or contractions. Always consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program during pregnancy.

pelvicTilt.name: Pelvic Tilts
pelvicTilt.desc: Strengthen core and relieve lower back pressure

childPose.name: Child's Pose
childPose.desc: Gentle stretch for the entire back

piriformis.name: Piriformis Stretch
piriformis.desc: Relieve sciatic pain and hip tension

sideStretch.name: Side Body Stretch
sideStretch.desc: Stretch the sides of your torso

sleepOptimizer.title: AI Sleep Optimizer
sleepOptimizer.subtitle: Personalized sleep guidance for pregnancy
sleepOptimizer.sleepTip: 💡 Left-side sleeping is recommended after week 20 for optimal blood flow
sleepOptimizer.videosSubtitle: Expert tips for better sleep during pregnancy

weeklySummary.trackTip: Track your week to get personalized information about your baby's growth and your body changes
